raw.population: N: 70 (Japanese) Age: ~13±~8 (0.8-43.8) WT: ~34±~16 (8.5-80.2) [CYP2C19] Homozygous EM: 33 Heterozygous EM: 30 PM: 16 [CYP2C9] *1/*1: 74 *1/*3: 5
raw.objective: “to clarify whether CYP2C9 and/or CYP2C19 polymorphisms affect the pharmacokinetics of PB”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, AGE, SEX, PHB daily dose, CYP2C9 and CYP2C19 genotypes, AED coadministration, severe or profound mental retardation (SMID) Included: WT, CYP2C9 genotype, VPA and PHT coadministration, SMID
raw.observations: Mental retardation is correlated with a 15% drop in clearance. CYP2C9 does, but CYP2C19 does not influence PHB clearance. No GOF plots given, making evaluation of their fit difficult.
raw.structure: 1 compartment with first order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: CL/F=theta2*((WT/40)**theta3)*(theta4**CYP2C9)*(theta5**VPA)*(theta6**PHT) *(theta7**SMID)*(1+eta2) Vd/F=theta1*(1+eta1) KA=theta3 Cobs=Cipred+eps1 KA was not reported, here set to 2 /hour CYP2C9 is genetic polymorphism (CYP2C9*1*3=1, other=0) VPA is comedication with valproic acid (yes=1, no=0) PHT is comedication with phenytoin (yes=1, no=0) SMID is 1 if complications of severe or profound mental retardation with a signiﬁcant behavior impairment are presented, otherwise it is 0
raw.parameter_values: theta1=14.78 theta2=0.23 theta3=0.21 theta4=0.53 theta5=0.68 theta6=0.85 theta7=0.85 omega1=2.93 omega2=0.03 sigma1=12.2

extraction_target.title: Population estimation of the effects of cytochrome P450 2C9 and 2C19 polymorphisms on phenobarbital clearance in Japanese.
extraction_target.population: N: 70 (Japanese) Age: ~13±~8 (0.8-43.8) WT: ~34±~16 (8.5-80.2) [CYP2C19] Homozygous EM: 33 Heterozygous EM: 30 PM: 16 [CYP2C9] *1/*1: 74 *1/*3: 5

structural_model.description: 1 compartment with first order absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F=theta2*((WT/40)**theta3)*(theta4**CYP2C9)*(theta5**VPA)*(theta6**PHT) *(theta7**SMID)*(1+eta2) Vd/F=theta1*(1+eta1) KA=theta3 Cobs=Cipred+eps1 KA was not reported, here set to 2 /hour CYP2C9 is genetic polymorphism (CYP2C9*1*3=1, other=0) VPA is comedication with valproic acid (yes=1, no=0) PHT is comedication with phenytoin (yes=1, no=0) SMID is 1 if complications of severe or profound mental retardation with a signiﬁcant behavior impairment are presented, otherwise it is 0
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.population: N: 70 (Japanese) Age: ~13±~8 (0.8-43.8) WT: ~34±~16 (8.5-80.2) [CYP2C19] Homozygous EM: 33 Heterozygous EM: 30 PM: 16 [CYP2C9] *1/*1: 74 *1/*3: 5
study.objective: “to clarify whether CYP2C9 and/or CYP2C19 polymorphisms affect the pharmacokinetics of PB”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, AGE, SEX, PHB daily dose, CYP2C9 and CYP2C19 genotypes, AED coadministration, severe or profound mental retardation (SMID) Included: WT, CYP2C9 genotype, VPA and PHT coadministration, SMID

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
